DISCOVERY, 302, PLAINTIFF MARILYN FISHER'S MOTION TO COMPEL INSPECTION OF PROPERTY AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS SET FOR MAY 31, 2011 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 302 IS OFF CALENDAR PER REQUEST OF THE MOVING PARTY.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARIANNE ADES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THERE WAS A DEFECT AND WHETHER THE DEFECT CAUSED PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATIONS OF JAY MANDELL AND JOHN DONLEY ARE OVERRULED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302/LMG) JUDGE: LORETTA M. MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the court orders the case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (610)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-31-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MARIANNE ADES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff has raised triable issues of fact as to whether there was a defect and whether the defect caused plaintiff's injuries. Defendant's objections to the declarations of Jay Mandell and John Donley are overruled. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
